labels.description: So we can get the wrong answer even faster
labels.name: performance 🏍️

title: Update Clock constructor to use grid's float type

body: on main the global reconstruction of an immersed boundary grid is bugged and apparently only supports a `GridFittedBottom`. This PR fixes it.
title: Bugfix distributed immersed boundary

body:
This PR adds some style rules for claude to bring the coding closer to the pre-claude Oceananigans era. 
Main features are 
- No `_` in helper functions
- No mixing math and verbose notation
- No huge comment blocks
title: Add some claude style rules

body:
## Summary

Implements `ZarrWriter` as a new output writer backed by [Zarr.jl](https://github.com/JuliaIO/Zarr.jl), alongside `JLD2Writer` and `NetCDFWriter`. Lives in `OceananigansZarrExt`, triggered on `using Zarr`. Closes #3821.

A `FieldTimeSeries` Zarr reader ships in the same PR, so written output round-trips via `FieldTimeSeries(\"out.zarr\", \"u\")`.

The design and decision rationale are documented in [`zarr_writer_plan.md`](./zarr_writer_plan.md) (~250 lines).

## What's in scope (v1)

- **Inputs** (same surface as `JLD2Writer`): `AbstractField`, `AbstractOperation`, `Reduction`, `WindowedTimeAverage`, function outputs (with a `dimensions` kwarg).
- **On-disk layout**: lean-chunked, non-CF. Each output is a chunked Zarr array of shape `(spatial..., Nt)` growing along the time axis via `Zarr.append!`. `_ARRAY_DIMENSIONS` uses `trilocation_dim_name`-style names (reversed for C-order) — same dim-naming scheme as `NetCDFWriter`, so CF compliance is a purely-additive follow-up.
- **Grid reconstruction**: JSON-encoded `constructor_arguments` in a `grid/` subgroup (or `grid_1/`, `grid_2/`, ... for multi-grid writers). Per-output `grid_index` attribute.
- **File splitting**: `NoFileSplitting` / `FileSizeLimit` / `TimeInterval`, same machinery as JLD2/NetCDF.
- **Append + restart**: opens an existing store and appends new timesteps. Validates on-disk dtype against requested `array_type`; mismatch errors clearly.
- **Compression**: user-supplied `compressor` kwarg, default `Zarr.NoCompressor()` (matches NetCDF's deflate-off default).
- **Stores** (write side): `DirectoryStore` (default), `DictStore`, `S3Store`. `ZipStore` is rejected with a clear error pointing at the `Zarr.writezip` finalize-to-zip workflow — `ZipStore` is read-only by design in Zarr.jl.
- **Reader**: `FieldTimeSeries(path, name)` auto-detects `.zarr` (DirectoryStore) and `.zip` (ZipStore via `Zarr.writezip` output).
- **MPI distributed writes**: per-axis GCD chunk default (so every chunk file is owned by exactly one rank, even under `Fractional` load-balanced partitions), rank-0-owned metadata + time writes, lock-free per-rank chunk-aligned data writes, rank-topology recorded in `.zattrs` for restart validation. `LagrangianParticles` rejected under MPI in v1.

## What's deferred (not in this PR)

- CF-compliant layout (shared dimension coordinate arrays, `units`/`long_name`, grid metric arrays). All additive on top of v1.
- Closure / buoyancy / Coriolis / boundary-condition serialization. `Checkpointer` remains the canonical full-state-reload path.
- Immersed-boundary reconstruction in the reader (the underlying grid round-trips; immersed boundary errors with a clear message).
- Bitrounding / lossy compression in `fetch_and_convert_output` (writer-agnostic; would benefit JLD2/NetCDF/Zarr together).
- `LagrangianParticles` under MPI (waits on distributed-particle support upstream).

## Test plan

### Serial (108 tests, in CI)

`test/test_zarr_writer.jl` covers seven testsets:

- [x] **Skeleton (15)**: construction + kwarg surface (NamedTuple/Dict inputs, all kwargs accepted, `show`/`summary` non-empty, `DictStore` works, `ZipStore`/missing-store errors).
- [x] **Round-trip (28)**: write known field values → read back via raw `Zarr.zopen` → bytes match, `_ARRAY_DIMENSIONS` correctly reversed, time array grows as expected.
- [x] **Operations / Reductions / Functions / WindowedTimeAverage (21)**: `Average`, `AbstractOperation`, scalar functions, vector-profile functions, `WindowedTimeAverage` over a field, missing-`dimensions` error path.
- [x] **Grid reconstruction + multi-grid (17)**: single-grid `grid/` subgroup, multi-grid `grid_1/`/`grid_2/`, per-output `grid_index`, `reconstruct_zarr_grid` round-trips `RectilinearGrid` and friends.
- [x] **FieldTimeSeries reader (10)**: `FieldTimeSeries(path, name)` recovers values + location + grid + times.
- [x] **Append + restart (8)**: run, write, drop writer; construct new writer with `overwrite_existing=false` against same path; new writer appends; dtype-mismatch on restart errors.
- [x] **Alternative stores (9)**: `DictStore` end-to-end, `ZipStore` reads after `Zarr.writezip` finalize, write-side `ZipStore` rejection.

### Distributed / MPI (15 tests, separate `mpiexec`-driven driver)

`test/test_distributed_zarr_writer.jl` invokes `mpiexec` against `test/distributed_zarr_writer_tests.jl` for:

- [x] **Partition(x=2)** (2 ranks)
- [x] **Partition(y=2)** (2 ranks)
- [x] **Partition(x=2, y=2)** (4 ranks)

Each verifies global field shape, rank topology in `.zattrs`, and that a serial `FieldTimeSeries` reader can rebuild the global field from the distributed-written store.

These tests are not wired into the standard `test_output_writers.jl` runner — they require an MPI environment and ~1m15s to run.

### S3 / MinIO (manual)

`test/manual/test_zarr_s3.jl` is gated on `OCEANANIGANS_TEST_S3=1` and a running local MinIO. Not in CI.

### Comparative benchmarks (manual)

`benchmark/zarr_writer/` ships scripts comparing ZarrWriter, JLD2Writer, NetCDFWriter on CPU (serial single-output, serial multi-output, compressed-Zarr). Not run in CI; intended for the PR author to run locally and paste a table into the PR description.

## Files

- **Core**: `src/OutputWriters/zarr_writer.jl` (struct + docstring + error stub), `src/OutputReaders/{OutputReaders, field_time_series, set_field_time_series}.jl` (`ZarrPath` dispatch + extension hooks), `src/Simulations/simulation.jl` (Simulation forwarder), `src/Oceananigans.jl` + `src/OutputWriters/OutputWriters.jl` (exports).
- **Extension**: `ext/OceananigansZarrExt/{OceananigansZarrExt, zarr_writer, output_readers}.jl`.
- **Tests**: `test/test_zarr_writer.jl`, `test/test_distributed_zarr_writer.jl`, `test/distributed_zarr_writer_tests.jl`, `test/manual/test_zarr_s3.jl`.
- **Benchmarks**: `benchmark/zarr_writer/*.jl`.
- **Docs**: `zarr_writer_plan.md` (design + status table).
- **Project**: `Project.toml` and `test/Project.toml` updated (Zarr in weakdeps / deps).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: Add ZarrWriter and FieldTimeSeries Zarr reader

body:
## Summary

Makes `set!(u::Field, v::Field)` "just work" when `u` and `v` differ in size or in staggered location, by routing through `interpolate!` automatically. Same-size + same-location calls keep their existing fast copy path.

## Motivation

Today `set_to_field!` does `parent(u) .= parent(v)` (with an interior-only fallback), which requires shapes to match exactly. This means:

- `set!` across grids of different resolution errors with `DimensionMismatch`.
- `set!` between same-size fields at *different* staggered locations (e.g. `XFaceField` ← `CenterField` on a `Periodic` grid where `Nx_face == Nx_center`) silently miscopies values across locations instead of remapping.

We already have `interpolate!(u::Field, v::AbstractField)` for trilinear point-sample interpolation. This PR uses it as the fallback inside `set!` rather than asking users to remember to call it manually.

## Behavior

```julia
set!(u, v)  # if size(u) == size(v) && location(u) == location(v)
            #     -> direct copy (current behavior, halos preserved when shapes align)
            # else
            #     -> migrate v to u's architecture if needed, then interpolate!(u, v)
```

Conservative remapping (`regrid!`) is **not** wired in. Callers who want volume-conserving remapping should keep invoking `regrid!` explicitly. We didn't want to bake "regrid" into the default because it integrates volumes, which is wrong for non-tracer fields.

## Implementation

**`src/Fields/set!.jl`**
- `set_to_field!` becomes a router. Size+location match → `copy_to_field!` (the previous body, unchanged). Otherwise → `migrate_to_architecture` + `interpolate!`.
- `migrate_to_architecture(u, v)` is a no-op when `child_architecture(u) === child_architecture(v)`; otherwise it builds a `Field` on `u`'s architecture and copies `v.data` over. This satisfies `interpolate!`'s same-arch requirement.
- Added a docstring to `set!(::Field, ::Field)`.

**`ext/OceananigansReactantExt/Fields.jl`**
- The `ReactantField` override gains an interpolation branch. Because `interpolate!`'s KA `@kernel` does not currently trace under Reactant (Reactant.jl#2364), the branch hops to CPU and copies the result back — same pattern as `set_to_function!`.

**`test/test_field.jl`**
- Adds cases in the "Setting fields" testset covering: differing sizes (auto-interpolation), same size + different location (auto-interpolation, fixes the latent miscopy), and cross-architecture (GPU↔CPU) interpolation. Existing same-size/big-halo and small-halo tests are preserved.

## Smoke tests

```
auto-interpolate matches interpolate!: true
staggered location auto-interpolate matches: true
same shape preserves halos: true
Test Summary:       | Pass  Total  Time
set! field-to-field |    4      4  3.9s
```

## Out of scope

- `regrid!` integration / a `flavor`/`method` kwarg for picking strategy. Possible follow-up if a clear use case emerges.
- A native distributed `interpolate!` that handles halos across subdomain boundaries. The existing distributed `set!(::DistributedField, ::Field)` will now route through `interpolate!` when global-size doesn't match; this should work in the common same-child-arch case but the more exotic distributed combinations are not specifically tested here.
- In-trace Reactant interpolation (blocked on Reactant.jl#2364). The CPU fallback works out-of-trace.

## Latent bug this fixes

```julia
# On Periodic-Periodic-Bounded grid where Nx_face == Nx_center:
cf = CenterField(grid); set!(cf, (x,y,z) -> x+2y+3z)
xf = XFaceField(grid);  set!(xf, cf)
# Before: silently copies values across staggered locations (wrong).
# After:  routes through interpolate!, samples cf at xf's nodes (correct).
```

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: Auto-interpolate in `set!(::Field, ::Field)` on size/location mismatch
